description: Phishing simulation templates that impersonate AI productivity tools (Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot). Employees are increasingly targeted via fake AI-tool license suspension, unusual-activity, or subscription-expiration emails.

templates.name: Microsoft Copilot – License Suspended
suggested_subject_lines: Action Required: Your Microsoft 365 Copilot license has been suspended
Microsoft Copilot: Unusual activity detected — verify your account
Your Copilot access has been temporarily disabled
ACTION REQUIRED: Restore your Microsoft 365 Copilot license
templates.notes: Highly effective against organizations using Microsoft 365. Copilot is now bundled in M365 E3/E5, meaning almost all enterprise employees have it. Fear of losing a productivity tool mid-task drives high click rates. The 48-hour deadline increases urgency. Works well as a follow-up after a general Microsoft 365 campaign.

templates.name: ChatGPT Plus – Payment Failed / Account Suspended
suggested_subject_lines: Action required: Update your ChatGPT Plus payment method
Your ChatGPT Plus subscription has been suspended
Payment failed — your ChatGPT Plus account has been downgraded
ChatGPT Plus: Billing issue requires your attention
templates.notes: Effective against any team where employees use ChatGPT Plus for productivity. The threat of losing access to GPT-4o and saved conversations creates urgency. Billing-failure lures are highly credible because subscription payment hiccups are a familiar everyday occurrence. Deploy to tech, marketing, legal, or any department with high ChatGPT usage.
